comment_text:
People are calling this the biggest movie in the world, but I don’t see the hype because it was frankly kind of a snoozefest. It’s nonstop chatter with all the action only happening for the sake of the music. As awesome as the characters, the music, and the overall concept for this movie are, and as beautiful as its representation of the culture of K-Pop is, at its core, it’s really nothing much to celebrate.

The biggest issue I take with it is how forced Rumi’s half demon lineage is. And by “forced”, I mean derivative”. It doesn’t feel like a genuine character trait so much as it looks more like a page ripped out from Firebreather’s playbook 

PS, Is NOBODY going to talk about how Mira looks almost exactly like Frieda from Happily n’Ever After?!

Maggie Kang, you're not just a director; you're a storyteller who has the power to touch hearts and souls. Please, use that power to bring Jinu back to us. Bring him back, and let us experience the magic of 'KPop Demon Hunters' once again.

Hear me out. KPop Demon Hunters sequel(s), but since "protecting the world" was mentioned in the beginning, bring to light the music of the world protecting from the demons of world cultures. A Legacy agreed each culture needed to protect their own because of certain aspects, but a demonic force appears that is so strong, only the unification of the world's demon hunters can subdue it. i.e. Baby Metal collaborations with so many other bands from different cultures. They have, as far as I have seen today, collaborated with Bloodywood (Indian), Electric Callboy (Germany), Slaughter to Prevail (Russia), Bring Me the Horizon (British), Tom Morello (of Rage Against the Machine/American-Italian/Irish), and Lil Uzi Vert (African). Using the ethnic/cultural sounds would be phenomenal in a world battling against their own demon worlds. 
We need the world, not just the mentioned.

comment_text:
There is a good movie for understanding Korean folk beliefs - shamanism. Along With the Gods: The Two Worlds. (2017) and Along With the Gods 2. The traditional meaning of the Huntrix is shaman. A shaman plays a role in connecting the human world with the afterlife. The being who governs the afterlife (heaven/hell) is OKWHANG SANGJE(옥황상제). SAJA is a being who guides the souls of the dead to the afterlife according to the command of the OKWHANG SANGJE(옥황상제).
In Korean folk beliefs, when human beings die, they follow the SAJA to the afterlife and are judged for what they have done in the human world for 49 days.
So, even in modern times, non-Christians hold a memorial service on the 49th day of their family's death.

comment_text:
Someone expressed "K/Da" as a short form for Korean Drama — I think that's such a creative expression. And what I want to introduce is the characteristics of the tiger in the Joseon Dynasty.
In the late Joseon period, as royal court paintings transitioned into folk paintings (minhwa), the tiger (Derpy)began to be depicted as a foolish character alongside a proud magpie(Sussie) often used to satirize society.
Although tigers were feared for attacking and eating people—leading to tiger hunts—they were also seen as “san-gun,” or "mountain Lords" believed to protect villages.

video_title: KPop Demon Hunters | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
This movie is a gem. The blend of the writing and visuals is top-tier. A great story where it doesnt hit you over the head with the writer's personal story. Instead, we get universal themes that all of us can relate to; a story of shame, idolatry,  and the connection of people through music. Speaking of music,  they did a great job of writing music that helped move the plot along and hot dayum, some were absolute earworms. The visuals, dear lord! The artists really made 2d art POP with both style and color, a treat for sure. This movie is absolutely rewatchable, the amount of times Ive watched it in full and in bits is obscenely gross, LOL. I could extol how great this movie is on ALL different levels ad nauseam but that would just get boring. Who wants that?
If you have not watch this film, I highly recommend you do so.
